page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by custom 64-bit ARMv9 AWS Graviton processors using the Neoverse-V3 model. It features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, ensuring no resource sharing between virtual CPUs. The memory configuration consists of 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the m9gd.4xlarge demonstrates strong memory and compression capabilities. The Passmark memory score ranks in the top tier, supported by a cached small-block read bandwidth of 2111.83 GB/sec and a large-block write bandwidth of 78.5 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression both achieve top-tier performance at 36.48 GB/sec and 17.31 GB/sec respectively. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput are also strong. CPU-bound tasks, such as stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests, generally register in the average tier, though the Passmark physics test and database operations show strong performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture combined with dedicated physical cores and integrated NVMe SSD storage delivers high resource density and strong performance-per-resource metrics for general-purpose workloads. The lack of hyperthreading means each of the 16 vCPUs maps to a dedicated physical core, reducing resource contention. This hardware profile is well-suited for database operations, web serving, compression tasks, and medium-scale LLM inference, where the local NVMe storage and high memory bandwidth can be fully utilized.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store, and an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, all running on the Nitro hypervisor. Lacking hyperthreading, the instance provides 16 dedicated physical cores. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory performance, with a high Passmark memory score and 2111.83 GB/sec cached read bandwidth, alongside top-tier single-threaded compression speeds. It also demonstrates strong performance in database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression. This resource profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring balanced compute, fast local storage, and high memory throughput. It is highly suitable for database hosting, web serving, data compression, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V3 cores, 64 GB of RAM, a 950 GB NVMe SSD, and top-tier memory and compression performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD for balanced workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 physical cores at 2.8 GHz.
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store.
Top-tier memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression performance.
Strong performance in database operations and static web serving.
Delivers 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
